ticket.title: Container-aware pickup prompts (epics + relay-with-subtickets)
handoff: Design: container tickets (relays with live sub-tickets; epics with live child relays) should be 'watering hole' prompts — a fresh agent arriving at an epic or relay sees the child list, is told they can claim sub-tickets sequentially, and gets pointed at the next live child. Individual sub-ticket prompt buttons still work independently; this is the alternate path where the user keeps coming back to the parent and lets the prompt pick the next piece of work. Current state: relay-with-subtickets partially does this via to_prompt_with_context (ticket.rs:635-674), but epics get the same flat treatment with Rule08 wording that doesn't quite fit child-relay semantics. Epic prompts have no per-child open-subticket counts and no 'start with next live relay' pointer.

Implemented container-aware pickup prompts. Added PromptContext + ChildLiveCounts types and TicketBoard::build_prompt_context helper (ticket.rs). Refactored Ticket::to_prompt_with_ctx as the new builder with backward-compat shims (to_prompt, to_prompt_with_context). Wired CLI main.rs to use build_prompt_context.

Four concrete fixes from the R011 case: (1) picker now picks earliest-LIVE child by ID — includes Claimed/InProgress, with status-aware verbs ('claim' / 'move active' / 'continue via --prompt'); (2) sub-ticket prompts inherit parent gotchas ('## ⚠ Gotchas inherited from <parent>') and parent verify smoke ('## Verification inherited from <parent>'); (3) relay-with-subtickets hoists the sub-tickets section above next_steps and relabels next_steps as 'Follow-on spawns (not the baton — see sub-tickets above)'; (4) epics render 'Child relays (watering hole — work one at a time)' with per-child grandchild counts ('2 open · 1 in-flight · 0 handoff') via two-level walk.

Added 5 new tests in ticket.rs: test_picker_picks_in_progress_child_not_next_fresh, test_subticket_inherits_parent_gotchas_and_verify, test_relay_with_children_hoists_subtickets_and_relabels_next, test_epic_prompt_shows_child_relays_with_grandchild_counts, test_flat_ticket_unchanged. All 26 ticket tests pass; full workspace suite clean.
next_steps: Extend to_prompt_with_context (or add a container variant) so epics walk one level deeper: list child relays, for each show count of its own open/handoff sub-tickets, and point at the first child relay that has claimable work.

Adjust wording: for relay-with-subtickets keep Rule08 cycle; for epics frame it as 'pick next live child relay, work its sub-tickets, come back'. Make the 'watering hole' re-entry pattern explicit so users know they can repeatedly prompt the container.

Consider container-ticket metadata beyond raw children: e.g. progress (N of M sub-tickets archived), last-touched child, which child is currently in-progress. Surface in the prompt so the agent sees state without opening the UI.
Wire through CLI (main.rs:4004-4019) — today it filters by direct parent match only; for an epic we need two-level filtering or a recursive walk.

BUG: next-live picker in ticket.rs:660-667 filters for Open|Handoff only — skips InProgress children. Real-world hit: R011 relay pointed at R011-T2 while R011-T1 was in-progress with open work. Fix: include InProgress (and Claimed) so the picker picks earliest-live by ID, not earliest-fresh.

Subticket prompts are starved of inherited parent context. When a ticket has a parent relay, its prompt should inherit the parent's gotchas (parser traps, etc), the most-recent completed-work summary (so adjacent subtickets' work shows up), and the parent's combined cargo smoke — otherwise each subticket pickup relearns gotchas the relay already paid for. See real example: R011-T1 had no gotchas even though Koda arm-syntax/tuple-lambda traps directly apply.

When a relay has live children, its own `next` list is usually forward-spawn noise (follow-on tickets to file, not the baton). De-emphasize or relabel it — e.g. 'Follow-on spawns' section rendered after sub-tickets, with the sub-ticket cycle as THE next action. Real example: R011's `next` listed three follow-on tickets (drum_machine, framework rule, doc retarget) which had nothing to do with continuing R011-T1/T2/T3.
verify: rs-hack board tickets --prompt <epic-id> shows child relays with open-subticket counts + next-live pointer
rs-hack board tickets --prompt <relay-id> with an InProgress child points at that child, not the next Open one
rs-hack board tickets --prompt <subticket-id> inherits parent relay's gotchas + verify smoke test

sourceLines: //! @hack:relay(R001, "hack-board: two-noun model")
//! @hack:kind(epic)
//! @hack:status(in-progress)
//! @hack:assignee(agent:claude)
//! @hack:phase(P1)
//! @hack:handoff("Refactored to two-noun model: Ticket + Relay. Feature/Bug/Task collapsed into Ticket with kind tag. Story/Epic replaced by Relay parent chain.")
//! @hack:verify("cargo test -p rs-hack-arch")
//! @hack:relay(R005, "Container-aware pickup prompts (epics + relay-with-subtickets)")
//! @hack:status(review)
//! @hack:assignee(agent:claude)

//! @hack:handoff("Design: container tickets (relays with live sub-tickets; epics with live child relays) should be 'watering hole' prompts — a fresh agent arriving at an epic or relay sees the child list, is told they can claim sub-tickets sequentially, and gets pointed at the next live child. Individual sub-ticket prompt buttons still work independently; this is the alternate path where the user keeps coming back to the parent and lets the prompt pick the next piece of work. Current state: relay-with-subtickets partially does this via to_prompt_with_context (ticket.rs:635-674), but epics get the same flat treatment with Rule08 wording that doesn't quite fit child-relay semantics. Epic prompts have no per-child open-subticket counts and no 'start with next live relay' pointer.")

//! @hack:next("Extend to_prompt_with_context (or add a container variant) so epics walk one level deeper: list child relays, for each show count of its own open/handoff sub-tickets, and point at the first child relay that has claimable work.")

//! @hack:next("Adjust wording: for relay-with-subtickets keep Rule08 cycle; for epics frame it as 'pick next live child relay, work its sub-tickets, come back'. Make the 'watering hole' re-entry pattern explicit so users know they can repeatedly prompt the container.")

//! @hack:next("Consider container-ticket metadata beyond raw children: e.g. progress (N of M sub-tickets archived), last-touched child, which child is currently in-progress. Surface in the prompt so the agent sees state without opening the UI.")
//! @hack:next("Wire through CLI (main.rs:4004-4019) — today it filters by direct parent match only; for an epic we need two-level filtering or a recursive walk.")

//! @hack:next("BUG: next-live picker in ticket.rs:660-667 filters for Open|Handoff only — skips InProgress children. Real-world hit: R011 relay pointed at R011-T2 while R011-T1 was in-progress with open work. Fix: include InProgress (and Claimed) so the picker picks earliest-live by ID, not earliest-fresh.")

//! @hack:next("Subticket prompts are starved of inherited parent context. When a ticket has a parent relay, its prompt should inherit the parent's gotchas (parser traps, etc), the most-recent completed-work summary (so adjacent subtickets' work shows up), and the parent's combined cargo smoke — otherwise each subticket pickup relearns gotchas the relay already paid for. See real example: R011-T1 had no gotchas even though Koda arm-syntax/tuple-lambda traps directly apply.")

//! @hack:next("When a relay has live children, its own `next` list is usually forward-spawn noise (follow-on tickets to file, not the baton). De-emphasize or relabel it — e.g. 'Follow-on spawns' section rendered after sub-tickets, with the sub-ticket cycle as THE next action. Real example: R011's `next` listed three follow-on tickets (drum_machine, framework rule, doc retarget) which had nothing to do with continuing R011-T1/T2/T3.")
//! @hack:verify("cargo test -p rs-hack-arch ticket::")
//! @hack:verify("rs-hack board tickets --prompt <epic-id> shows child relays with open-subticket counts + next-live pointer")
//! @hack:verify("rs-hack board tickets --prompt <relay-id> with an InProgress child points at that child, not the next Open one")
//! @hack:verify("rs-hack board tickets --prompt <subticket-id> inherits parent relay's gotchas + verify smoke test")

//! @hack:handoff("Implemented container-aware pickup prompts. Added PromptContext + ChildLiveCounts types and TicketBoard::build_prompt_context helper (ticket.rs). Refactored Ticket::to_prompt_with_ctx as the new builder with backward-compat shims (to_prompt, to_prompt_with_context). Wired CLI main.rs to use build_prompt_context.")

//! @hack:handoff("Four concrete fixes from the R011 case: (1) picker now picks earliest-LIVE child by ID — includes Claimed/InProgress, with status-aware verbs ('claim' / 'move active' / 'continue via --prompt'); (2) sub-ticket prompts inherit parent gotchas ('## ⚠ Gotchas inherited from <parent>') and parent verify smoke ('## Verification inherited from <parent>'); (3) relay-with-subtickets hoists the sub-tickets section above next_steps and relabels next_steps as 'Follow-on spawns (not the baton — see sub-tickets above)'; (4) epics render 'Child relays (watering hole — work one at a time)' with per-child grandchild counts ('2 open · 1 in-flight · 0 handoff') via two-level walk.")

//! @hack:handoff("Added 5 new tests in ticket.rs: test_picker_picks_in_progress_child_not_next_fresh, test_subticket_inherits_parent_gotchas_and_verify, test_relay_with_children_hoists_subtickets_and_relabels_next, test_epic_prompt_shows_child_relays_with_grandchild_counts, test_flat_ticket_unchanged. All 26 ticket tests pass; full workspace suite clean.")
